sourceMovie.title: Flatliners
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Flatliners (1990) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Joel Schumacher's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The drama and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Re-Animator
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Flatliners clicked for you, Re-Animator (1985) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Discovery
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Flatliners clicked for you, The Discovery (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and science fiction, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Flatliners?
faq.answer: Try Re-Animator, The Discovery, The Endless—each shares DNA with Flatliners while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Flatliners?
faq.answer: Flatliners (1990) is best described as Drama, Science Fiction, Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Flatliners worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
